name: Hoisin Chicken Stir Fry
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut skinned and boneless chicken breasts into bite-sized pieces. In a plastic bag, mix slightly beaten egg whites with 1 teaspoon cornstarch, soy sauce, and black pepper. Add the chicken, seal the bag, and refrigerate for up to 1 hour, turning the bag occasionally.
2. Cut both peppers into thin strips. Slice the onion very thin and separate it into rings. In a bowl, mix 1 tablespoon cornstarch with water, sugar, and sesame oil.
3. Heat the wok until very hot. Pour oil down the sides of the wok and swirl to coat all surfaces. Add the onion, garlic, and ginger. Stir-fry for 1-2 minutes until the onion is light brown and softened. Add the chicken and stir-fry until the pieces turn white. Add Hoisin sauce and chili paste; stir for about 30-45 seconds.
4. Stir in the chicken broth and heat to boiling. Add the green and red pepper strips; stir-fry for about 1 minute more. Stir in the cornstarch mixture and continue cooking until thickened. If desired, sprinkle with roasted peanuts.
